care.summary: A stunning variegated Monstera with white patches on dark green leaves. Highly sought-after collector aroid that requires bright indirect light and high humidity to maintain its variegation.
care.light: Bright, indirect light. Direct sun can burn the white parts of the leaves. Lower light may cause loss of variegation.
care.water: Water when the top 2-3 inches of soil feel dry. Reduce watering in winter. Ensure pot has drainage.
care.soil: Well-draining aroid mix: peat or coco coir, perlite, and orchid bark. Avoid heavy, water-retentive soil.
care.humidity: High humidity (60% or higher). Use a humidifier, pebble tray, or group with other plants. Brown leaf edges indicate low humidity.
care.fertilizer: Balanced liquid fertilizer (e.g., 20-20-20) diluted to half strength every 4 weeks during spring and summer. Do not fertilize in winter.
care.toxicity: Toxic to pets and humans if ingested. Contains insoluble calcium oxalate crystals. Keep out of reach of children and animals.
common_issues: Leaf browning or crispy edges due to low humidity or underwatering
Loss of variegation (reverting to all green) from insufficient light
Root rot from overwatering or poor drainage
Mealybugs and spider mites in dry indoor air
tips: Use a moss pole or trellis to support climbing growth; larger leaves develop as it climbs
Wipe leaves gently with a damp cloth to remove dust and prevent pest buildup
Rotate the plant every few weeks to ensure even light exposure
Prune any fully green leaves to encourage variegated growth
